Original Article Title: How an image could compromise your Mac: understanding an ExifTool vulnerability (CVE-2026-3102)
Summary: A remote OS command injection vulnerability exists in ExifTool versions up to 13.49 on macOS, specifically within the SetMacOSTags function of the PNG File Parser. Attackers can exploit this flaw by manipulating the DateTimeOriginal argument.

Original Article Title: Inside agenteV2: How Brazilian Attackers Use Fake Court Summons to Steal Banking Credentials in Real Time
Summary: The agenteV2 stealer is a persistent WebSocket-based backdoor that uses a Pastebin dead-drop resolver to dynamically update its command-and-control (C2) infrastructure. The malware is delivered via phishing emails and provides attackers with real-time remote control capabilities, including screen streaming and interactive shell access.

Summary: PraisonAI contains a critical vulnerability, CVE-2026-44338, where the legacy API server defaults to disabling authentication and binding to all network interfaces. Original Article Title: Flash Alert: EtherRat and TukTuk C2 End in The Gentleman Ransomware

The malware employs obfuscated JavaScript payloads and leverages blockchain-based configuration retrieval to maintain persistence and rotate C2 infrastructure.

These campaigns utilize a variety of C2 domains and IP addresses to distribute commodity malware, including LummaStealer and PurpleFox, often disguised as legitimate services.

Original Article Title: Operation Road Trap: Fake toll and parking texts are spreading worldwide
Summary: Bitdefender Labs has identified a global smishing campaign targeting users with fake traffic and toll violation notices. These messages direct victims to malicious domains designed to steal personal information or deploy malware via dropper APKs.

Original Article Title: Steganography Secrets: Malware Hidden in Plain Sight
Summary: Threat actors are increasingly utilizing steganography to hide malicious payloads within image files hosted on legitimate platforms. Campaigns identified include the delivery of Remcos RAT and Agent Tesla Keylogger, with significant abuse of image hosting services to evade detection.

Original Article Title: Click, Install, Compromised: The New Wave of Zoom-Themed Attacks
Summary: Attackers are distributing malicious VBScript downloaders disguised as Zoom meeting invites to deploy ScreenConnect remote access software. The campaign utilizes specific domains and IP addresses to host the malicious payloads and facilitate command and control communication.

Original Article Title: JOMANGY: INJ3CTOR3’s Self-Healing FreePBX Toll Fraud Campaign
